sections.paragraph:
τεκνογονέω (s. next entry; Philippus Epigr. [I a.d.]: Anth. Pal. 9, 22, 4 [of an animal]) bear/beget children (Appian., Basil. 1a §5; Achmes 63, 10) 1 Ti 5:14; Dg 5:6 (pointing to a normal practice among Christians and in contrast to custom of infanticide; s. ῥίπτω 1).—Cp. DELG s.v. γίγνομαι; Frisk s.v. γονή. M-M.

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 994
